patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # RGE Handoff Document
 
+> **Snapshot**: 2026-06-04 (MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE — File/Edit menu items now show their keyboard accelerators, and the menu construction is its own submodule; the menu-deepening thread is closed): #304 (`284998e`, MENU-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY) makes the **File + Edit** menu items render their real keyboard accelerator next to the label — `Open…` Ctrl+O, `Save` Ctrl+S, `Save As New Project…` Ctrl+Shift+S, `Undo` Ctrl+Z, `Redo` Ctrl+Y — via egui's right-aligned `shortcut_text`, sourced from the existing `editor-ui::menus::Shortcut` substrate carried on `MenuEntry.shortcut` (the projection widened from `(label, Command)` to `(label, Option<accelerator-display>, Command)`; a `menu_item` render helper paints each `egui::Button` + optional `shortcut_text`). **Display-only:** clicking still dispatches through the host→shell FIFO — the accelerator is a passive hint, NO command-execution / keystroke-routing / crate-edge change. **File/Edit only (Play/View deferred, `None`):** the shown values mirror the live, executing editor-shell keystroke routing (`EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm), pinned by host tests; the host CANNOT import editor-shell (reverse edge), so `MenuEntry.shortcut` (editor-ui) is the designated home and the W08 accelerator-EXECUTION work will unify the two via the resolved `AcceleratorTable`. Play's only shortcut data is the play-toolbar's ADVISORY `F5/Esc` hints, which don't match the real Space/Escape PIE binds — surfacing them would open the advisory-vs-real accelerator/execution conflict this beat avoids; View ▸ Reset Camera has no binding. **Then #305 (`892a0ad`, EGUIHOST-MENU-EXTRACTION) extracted the menu-construction block** (the four extension-point consts + `build_main_menu_entries` + `play_item_enabled` + `menu_item`) VERBATIM out of `editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` into a new `menu.rs` submodule — behaviour-identical (render path + every test assertion byte-unchanged) — dropping lib.rs 1062 → 836 lines and **RETIRING the `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation** (0 markers crate-wide; mirrors EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION #301). This narrows the prior snapshot's "accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population" deferred line — the **DISPLAY** half shipped, for **File/Edit**. STILL DEFERRED: accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population (Play/View accelerator display deferred with it); the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve; dynamic toggle LABELS; plugin menu entries; generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO); dynamic enablement for the File / Edit / View menus. The PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
+
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-03 (PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE — Play-menu items now grey out per live PlayState; first menu-deepening beat past breadth): #302 (`9960b30`, PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-ENABLE) makes the Play menu items (Play / Pause / Stop / Step) **dynamically enabled** — greyed out (disabled-but-visible) when the current `PlayState` makes the transition a no-op, instead of always-clickable with a benign-swallowed no-op. Authority-clean: `PlayState` stays the sole owner of transition validity via new `can_play` / `can_pause` / `can_stop` / `can_step` queries (derived from the canonical state, pinned to the real `play()` / `pause()` / `stop()` / `step()` transitions by a consistency test); `EditorShell::menu_state_snapshot()` publishes those four booleans each frame through a new latest-only `MenuStateHandoff` (= `Handoff<MenuStateSnapshot>`, the **third** snapshot handoff alongside Inspector / SaveStatus); the host's `render` acquires it and `add_enabled`s each Play item via `play_item_enabled` (a pure boolean router — NO validity rule re-encoded host-side). `MenuStateSnapshot` is a new editor-state **observation aggregator** (sibling to `SaveStatusSnapshot`, NOT a 6th coordination category, so `editor-state-ownership` stays green). **This is a BESPOKE per-frame enablement channel, NOT the W08 registry `Predicate` / per-frame `resolve` path (which remains unbuilt).** Behaviour: Editing → only Play enabled; Playing → Pause + Stop; Paused → all four; File / Edit / View items stay unconditionally enabled (Play-only beat). This narrows the A4 snapshot's "dynamic predicates / per-frame re-resolve (no dynamic toggle labels yet)" deferred line below. STILL DEFERRED: the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame registry re-resolve (menu set/visibility via `Predicate::Closure`); dynamic toggle LABELS (a Play⇄Pause label, a camera-state-aware View item — needs per-frame label re-resolve); accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population; plugin menu entries; generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO); and dynamic enablement for the File / Edit / View menus. The A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.
 
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-03 (MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A4 — View menu landed; the menu-breadth arc File / Edit / Play / View is COMPLETE): #299 (`be4896a`, VIEWMENU-RESETCAMERA-A4) added a **View** menu (Reset Camera) — the fourth and final standard menu — via the same W08 `MenuRegistry` path. **Unlike the prior three (pure wiring), View needed a NEW runtime action:** `EguiHost::new` declares a fourth extension point `editor.main_menu.view` and registers Reset Camera (`Command::ResetCamera`); the editor-shell drain routes it to the new **infallible** `EditorShell::reset_camera`, which reframes `editor_camera` to the isometric view of the live scene's AABB union (new `current_scene_bounds` helper sourcing live meshes the same way as the render path — prebuilt meshes, else the CAD projection — and reusing `compute_aabb_union` + `isometric_camera_for_bounds`), falling back to the default pose when nothing is frameable. Product semantics (owner-confirmed): frame the scene, mirroring the constructor's auto-frame, not a fixed default-pose jump. (The View additions tipped `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` past 1000L — production ~913L + inline tests — so a `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation was added per the `host.rs` precedent; a flagged **EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION** follow-up will extract the inline test module and retire it.) This supersedes the A3 snapshot below, which listed View as the lone remaining standard menu. STILL DEFERRED (NO standard menus remain — the breadth arc is closed): registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO), accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population, plugin entries, dynamic predicates, and per-frame re-resolve (no dynamic toggle labels — e.g. Play⇄Pause, or a camera-state-aware View item — yet). The A3 / A2 / A1 snapshots below and older dated lines are historical and are not rewritten in place.

@@ -1658,3 +1658,5 @@ ew / partial / is_full_revolution / existing geometry tests. **Critical design c
 2026-06-03 (MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A4 — planning-substrate hygiene after A4, docs-only). Forward-only reconciliation after #299 (`be4896a`, VIEWMENU-RESETCAMERA-A4) added the **View** menu (Reset Camera), closing the menu-breadth arc — File (A1) / Edit (A2) / Play (A3) / View (A4) all build from the one shared `MenuRegistry`. Unlike A1–A3 (pure wiring), View needed a NEW runtime action: `build_main_menu_entries` declares a fourth point `editor.main_menu.view` + registers `Command::ResetCamera` (the host now resolves all four points, caches four lists; `render` paints a fourth `menu_button("View")`); `editor-shell` `drain_and_route_menu_commands` routes `Command::ResetCamera` to the new infallible `EditorShell::reset_camera`, which reframes `editor_camera` to `isometric_camera_for_bounds(current_scene_bounds)` (live scene AABB union sourced as in render_path Step 6 — prebuilt meshes, else the CAD projection mesh), falling back to `EditorCameraState::default()` when nothing is frameable. Product semantics (owner-confirmed): frame the scene, not a fixed default-pose jump. This entry + new top snapshots in `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` + a new `plans/BASELINE.md` A4 subsection supersede the now-stale "View is the lone remaining standard menu / pending feature beat" framing from the A3 reconcile (which predated A4). **Split-exemption:** the View additions tipped `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` past 1000L (~913L production + inline tests), so a `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation was added per the `host.rs` precedent; a flagged EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION follow-up will extract the inline test module and retire it. **Menu rustdoc already current:** #299 (incl. its `6cd222d` correction round) widened the `editor-egui-host` menu rustdoc/comments to "File + Edit + Play + View", so — like A3 — this docs reconcile touches NO source comments and NO `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md` counts. **Still deferred (NO standard menus remain):** generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still use the FIFO), accelerator-table execution/display/conflict, plugin entries, dynamic predicates, per-frame re-resolve (including dynamic toggle labels). **History preserved:** the A3 (#298) reconcile entry above + all older dated entries are byte-identical (forward-only). **Scope:** docs-only — edits confined to `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing / rustdoc / lint-doc change. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean; `git diff --stat be4896a..HEAD` = exactly the 4 docs; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0 (`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`, 10/10 architecture lints 0 violations — Markdown-only change, no Rust touched). **Three required references present in this entry:** `plans/BASELINE.md` new "2026-06-03 — View menu now registry-produced; menu-breadth arc complete (#299 A4)" subsection, the merged PR #299 (`be4896a`), and `ai_handoffs/MENUARC-DOC-RECONCILE-A4_TASK_2026-06-03_14-23-21+0300.md`.
 
 2026-06-03 (PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE — planning-substrate hygiene after the first menu-deepening beat, docs-only). Forward-only reconciliation after #302 (`9960b30`, PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-ENABLE) made the Play menu items (Play / Pause / Stop / Step) dynamically enabled — greyed out when the current `PlayState` makes the transition a no-op. Authority-clean: `PlayState::can_play` / `can_pause` / `can_stop` / `can_step` (new, pinned to the real transitions by a consistency test) → `EditorShell::menu_state_snapshot()` publishes four booleans each frame via a new latest-only `MenuStateHandoff` (= `Handoff<MenuStateSnapshot>`, the third snapshot handoff) → the host `add_enabled`s each Play item via `play_item_enabled` (a pure boolean router). `MenuStateSnapshot` is a new editor-state observation aggregator (sibling to `SaveStatusSnapshot`, not a 6th coordination category — `editor-state-ownership` green). This entry + new top snapshots in `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` + a new `plans/BASELINE.md` subsection narrow the A4 reconcile's "dynamic predicates / per-frame re-resolve (no dynamic toggle labels yet)" deferred line. **Bespoke, not W08:** this is a dedicated per-frame enablement channel, NOT the W08 registry `Predicate` / `resolve` path (still unbuilt). **Menu / handoff / snapshot rustdoc already current:** #302 itself (incl. its four stale-prose correction rounds) widened the handoff-set + snapshot-set listings to include the new MenuState members, so this docs reconcile touches NO source comments and NO `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md` counts. **Still deferred (narrowed):** the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve (menu set/visibility); dynamic toggle labels (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View); accelerator-table execution/display/conflict; plugin entries; generalized registry/accelerator execution (clicks still use the FIFO); dynamic enablement for File / Edit / View (Play-only beat). **History preserved:** the A4 (#300) reconcile entry above + all older dated entries are byte-identical (forward-only). **Scope:** docs-only — edits confined to `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing / rustdoc / lint-doc change. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean; `git diff --stat 9960b30..HEAD` = exactly the 4 docs; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0 (`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`, 10/10 architecture lints 0 violations — Markdown-only change, no Rust touched). **Three required references present in this entry:** `plans/BASELINE.md` new "2026-06-03 — Play-menu items now dynamically enabled per live PlayState (#302)" subsection, the merged PR #302 (`9960b30`), and `ai_handoffs/PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE_TASK_2026-06-03_23-15-10+0300.md`.
+
+2026-06-04 (MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE — planning-substrate hygiene after the File/Edit accelerator beat + the menu-module extraction, docs-only). Forward-only reconciliation after #304 (`284998e`, MENU-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY) made File + Edit menu items show their real keyboard accelerator (Open Ctrl+O / Save Ctrl+S / Save-As Ctrl+Shift+S / Undo Ctrl+Z / Redo Ctrl+Y) via egui `shortcut_text`, sourced from the existing `editor-ui::menus::Shortcut` substrate on `MenuEntry.shortcut` (projection widened `(label, Command)` → `(label, Option<accelerator-display>, Command)`; a `menu_item` render helper paints each button + optional `shortcut_text`); and after #305 (`892a0ad`, EGUIHOST-MENU-EXTRACTION) extracted the menu-construction block (the four extension-point consts + `build_main_menu_entries` + `play_item_enabled` + `menu_item`) VERBATIM into a new `editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs` submodule (behaviour-identical), dropping lib.rs 1062 → 836 lines and retiring the `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation #304 re-added (0 markers crate-wide). This entry + new top snapshots in `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` + a new `plans/BASELINE.md` subsection narrow the prior reconciles' "accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population" deferred line — the DISPLAY half shipped, for File/Edit. **Display-only + authority-clean:** clicks still dispatch through the host→shell FIFO (no command-execution / keystroke-routing / crate-edge change); the shown values mirror the live `EditorKeyCommand` + `Ctrl+O` routing (the host cannot import editor-shell), pinned by host tests, with W08 accelerator EXECUTION to unify via the `AcceleratorTable`. **File/Edit only:** Play's only shortcut data is the play-toolbar's advisory `F5/Esc` hints (≠ the real Space/Escape PIE binds — surfacing them opens the advisory-vs-real conflict this beat avoids); View ▸ Reset Camera has no binding. **#304 had one Codex correction round** (a stale "same tests" claim in the extracted `menu_tests.rs` doc); **#305 was a pure behaviour-identical move** mirroring EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION (#301). **Menu / accelerator rustdoc already current:** #304 + #305 made the rustdoc current themselves (incl. #304's stale-prose round), so this docs reconcile touches NO source comments and NO `docs/§18/ARCHITECTURE_LINTS.md` counts. **Still deferred (narrowed):** accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population (display shipped for File/Edit only; Play/View display deferred); W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve; dynamic toggle labels; plugin entries; generalized registry/accelerator execution (clicks still use the FIFO). **History preserved:** the PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE (#302) entry above + all older dated entries are byte-identical (forward-only). **Scope:** docs-only — edits confined to `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`; no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo / routing / rustdoc / lint-doc change. **Verification:** `git diff --check` clean; `git diff --stat 892a0ad..HEAD` = exactly the 4 docs; `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exit 0 (`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`, 10/10 architecture lints 0 violations — Markdown-only change, no Rust touched). **Three required references present in this entry:** `plans/BASELINE.md` new "2026-06-04 — File/Edit menu accelerators shown; menu construction extracted to a submodule (#304 + #305)" subsection, the merged PRs #304 (`284998e`) + #305 (`892a0ad`), and `ai_handoffs/MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE_TASK_2026-06-04_02-15-09+0300.md`.

@@ -558,6 +558,29 @@ Until **at least one** of those fires, treat the reflection substrate as observe
 4. Cross-check the editor's call graph against the `CommandBus::submit` / `Action::apply` / `Action::revert` signatures to determine whether user-visible CAD mutations can flow through the existing bus.
 5. Test inventory across `editor-*` (`#[test]` count + integration vs unit breakdown + workflow coverage).
 
+### 2026-06-04 — File/Edit menu accelerators shown; menu construction extracted to a submodule (#304 + #305)
+
+**Forward-only follow-up (MENU-SHORTCUT-DOC-RECONCILE).** Narrows the prior subsections' "accelerator-table execution/display/conflict population … unbuilt" line: per-item accelerator DISPLAY shipped for File/Edit. Records the two merged PRs that close the menu-deepening thread.
+
+**Now shipped — File/Edit accelerator display (#304, `284998e`, MENU-SHORTCUT-DISPLAY).** File + Edit menu items render their real keyboard accelerator next to the label via egui `shortcut_text`:
+- `crates/editor-egui-host` attaches `MenuEntry::with_shortcut(Shortcut::new(..))` to the 3 File + 2 Edit entries (Open Ctrl+O / Save Ctrl+S / Save-As Ctrl+Shift+S / Undo Ctrl+Z / Redo Ctrl+Y); the projection widened `(label, Command)` → `(label, Option<accelerator-display>, Command)`, sourced straight from each resolved `MenuEntry.shortcut` via `Shortcut::display`. A `menu_item` render helper builds each `egui::Button` + optional `shortcut_text`.
+- Display-only: clicks still dispatch through the host→shell FIFO; no command-execution / keystroke-routing / crate-edge change. Play/View project `None`.
+
+Authority: the shown values mirror the live, executing editor-shell routing (`EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` + the `Ctrl+O` `handle_open_request` arm), pinned by host tests; the host cannot import editor-shell (reverse edge), so `MenuEntry.shortcut` (editor-ui) is the designated accelerator home and the deferred W08 EXECUTION work unifies the two via the resolved `AcceleratorTable`.
+
+**File/Edit only — Play/View deferred.** Play's only shortcut data is the play-toolbar's ADVISORY `F5/Esc` hints, which don't match the real Space/Escape PIE binds; surfacing them would open the advisory-vs-real accelerator/execution conflict this beat avoids. View ▸ Reset Camera has no keystroke binding.
+
+**Now retired — the split-cap exemption (#305, `892a0ad`, EGUIHOST-MENU-EXTRACTION).** #304's additions re-crossed the §1.3 Rule-3 1000-line cap on `editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`, so #304 re-added the `// SPLIT-EXEMPTION:` annotation (the same one #301 had retired). #305 then extracted the cohesive menu-construction block (the four extension-point consts + `build_main_menu_entries` + `play_item_enabled` + `menu_item`) VERBATIM into a new `menu.rs` submodule — behaviour-identical (render path + every test assertion byte-unchanged) — dropping lib.rs 1062 → 836 lines and RETIRING the annotation (0 markers crate-wide). Mirrors EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION (#301), which split the inline tests out as its own dispatch.
+
+**Still open — explicitly NOT closed here:**
+- accelerator-table EXECUTION + conflict population (this beat ships DISPLAY only, and only for File/Edit; Play/View accelerator display is deferred with the W08 execution work).
+- the W08 registry-driven dynamic predicates + per-frame re-resolve; dynamic toggle LABELS (Play⇄Pause, camera-state-aware View).
+- plugin menu entries + generalized registry/accelerator-driven command execution (clicks still flow through the host→shell FIFO).
+
+**Historical preservation.** The PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC / A4 / A3 / A2 / A1 subsections below and all earlier dated entries are preserved byte-identical; their "accelerator-table execution/display/conflict … unbuilt" lines are narrowed forward by this subsection (File/Edit display shipped), not rewritten in place. (The A4 subsection's flagged EGUIHOST-TEST-EXTRACTION follow-up landed as #301; the exemption it + #304 carried is now fully retired by #305.)
+
+**Scope:** docs only (`Status.md` + `HANDOFF.md` + `plans/BASELINE.md` + `change.md`); no Rust source-logic / test / Cargo change (the menu / accelerator rustdoc was made current inside #304 + #305 themselves).
+
 ### 2026-06-03 — Play-menu items now dynamically enabled per live PlayState (#302)
 
 **Forward-only follow-up (PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-DOC-RECONCILE).** Narrows the A4 subsection below, whose "dynamic predicates, and per-frame re-resolve … remain unbuilt" line is now partly superseded: per-frame Play-item ENABLEMENT shipped. #302 (`9960b30`, PLAYMENU-DYNAMIC-ENABLE) is the first menu-deepening beat past breadth.
